segments.text: Lord, remember David and all his self-denial.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He swore an oath to the Lord, he made a vow to the Mighty One of Jacob:
I will not enter my house or go to my bed,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will allow no sleep to my eyes or slumber to my eyelids,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: till I find a place for the Lord, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: We heard it in Ephrathah, we came upon it in the fields of Jaar:
Let us go to his dwelling place, let us worship at his footstool, saying,
Arise, Lord, and come to your resting place, you and the ark of your might.
segments.speaker: Worshippers

segments.text: May your priests be clothed with your righteousness; may your faithful people sing for joy.
segments.speaker: Worshippers

segments.text: For the sake of your servant David, do not reject your anointed one.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The Lord swore an oath to David, a sure oath he will not revoke:
One of your own descendants I will place on your throne.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If your sons keep my covenant and the statutes I teach them, then their sons will sit on your throne for ever and ever.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: For the Lord has chosen Zion, he has desired it for his dwelling, saying,
This is my resting place for ever and ever; here I will sit enthroned, for I have desired it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will bless her with abundant provisions; her poor I will satisfy with food.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will clothe her priests with salvation, and her faithful people will ever sing for joy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Here I will make a horn grow for David and set up a lamp for my anointed one.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will clothe his enemies with shame, but his head will be adorned with a radiant crown.
segments.speaker: The Lord
